communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All records relating to (1) the in-person visit, attendance, and filing of any police report, complaint, statement, or application/request for an Emergency Protective Order (EPO) by Dana Rees (also known as Dana Carole Hall) and/or her mother Emily Liebscher, at the North Hollywood Police Station on or about March 10, 2015, concerning Jonathan Rees aka Greg Ellis, including any and all documents, statements, allegations, audio recordings, video footage, body-worn camera recordings, station security/lobby camera footage, and other records of her visit and interactions with officers or staff; and (2) the subsequent LAPD response, detention, and service of an Emergency Protective Order involving Jonathan Rees aka Greg Ellis (including any suicide welfare check or domestic violence response) occurring on or about March 11, 2015 (in the early morning hours), at 4705 Ledge Ave, Toluca Lake, CA 91602.
Incident reports; complaint reports; aided reports; case reports; narrative reports; call-for-service records; CAD records; dispatch logs; event chronologies; dispatcher notes; unit histories; 911 call recordings; non-emergency call recordings; audio recordings; transcripts; transcript excerpts; call summaries; records reflecting the reporting party’s self-identification; stated name of the reporting party; stated relationship of the reporting party to the subject; callback number; ANI (Automatic Number Identification); caller ID; telephone number associated with the caller; records showing the date and time of the call; records showing the nature of the complaint; records showing the disposition of the call; records identifying responding personnel.
If any portion of these records is exempt from disclosure, I request that all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions be provided. For any material withheld or redacted, please provide the specific statutory basis for each denial or redaction.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Pete Odin
